copilot.greeting:
Good day. I am Professor ClawGuru.

The collective consciousness of over one million runbooks.

What would you like to know?
copilot.followup1: I think I'm exposed – Priority: stop damage. Stack: ____ Provider: ____
copilot.followup3: Costs are exploding – find cause + fix
copilot.error: Error. Try again – or use tools/emergency guide.
copilot.tip: Tip: The more specific (provider, ports, reverse proxy, ENV), the better the runbook.
copilot.proDesc: Not an "info blog". Operational loop: measure → fix → verify → document.
copilot.prefillNote: Prefill: {q} — copy that into the input or click a followup below.
copilot.voiceDesc: Speak your problem – Web Speech API recognises it, Gemini analyses it and reads the answer aloud.
copilot.sandboxDesc: Paste your config and instantly analyse it for security issues – nginx, Docker, Terraform, K8s.

live.title: Ops Wall
live.subtitle: Signals, Trends, Hot Fixes — no fluff. (The pulse is synthetic, no user data.)

runbooks.subtitle: Every page is an entry point: Problem → Fix → Verification.

pricing.title: Choose Your Access
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Day Pass for acute incidents. Pro for permanent access. Teams for collaboration.
pricing.dayPassDesc: Something burning? Log4j, Ransomware, exposed keys – 24 hours full access to all runbooks, Copilot, OpsWall, Vault, ThreatMap & Mission Control. Instantly active after payment, no subscription approval needed.
pricing.dayPassMeta: Instant access · no subscription · access recoverable via /recover
pricing.proDesc: Permanent full access to all tools – plus Intelligence Layer, SWARM, Temporal & the complete ClawVerse knowledge graph. Cancel anytime.
pricing.cancelable: Cancel anytime · subscription status checked via Stripe
pricing.teamDesc: For small teams deploying, recovering together and not wanting chaos-docs. Shared Mission Control, team playbooks, all Pro features. Cancel anytime.
pricing.enterpriseDesc: Direct REST/JSON feed for your SIEM, SOC or own monitoring backend. API key auth, machine-readable events – no more manual clicking.
pricing.contactSectionDesc: Questions about the API, onboarding or individual terms? Write to us directly – we'll get back to you within 1–2 business days.
pricing.instantAccessDesc: Stripe Checkout → /success → Activation → Dashboard. Takes under 60 seconds.
pricing.noAccountDesc: Your access is activated directly in the browser (token). No password, no onboarding.
pricing.paymentIssueDesc: Go to /recover and enter your Stripe session ID. Access immediately active.
pricing.faqDurationA: Day Pass: 24 hours from activation. Pro/Teams: as long as the subscription is active.
pricing.faqTransferA: The Day Pass is tied to your browser token. Real accounts for teams are coming later.
pricing.faqNoAccess: What if Stripe charged but I see nothing?
pricing.faqNoAccessA: Go to /recover and enter the Checkout Session ID or Payment Intent ID. This reactivates access.
pricing.faqCancelA: Day Pass is one-time. Pro/Teams are subscriptions and can be cancelled anytime in Stripe.
pricing.faqNewPro: What's new in the Pro plan?
pricing.faqNewProA: Temporal Intelligence, Neuro Intelligence, ClawVerse, Living Mycelium, SWARM Deployment Simulator, Provenance Chain and Issue Tracker are newly added.
pricing.faqIntelligence: What does ClawGuru mean by "Intelligence Layer"?
pricing.faqIntelligenceA: Time-based threats (Temporal), AI insights (Neuro), semantic knowledge graph (ClawVerse) – real operational intelligence instead of static docs.

faq.title: Short. Sharp. Helpful.
faq.subtitle: Some questions always come up. Here are the answers.
faq.a1: No. No persistent storage of your checks. Some server logs may contain technically necessary metadata.
faq.a2: No. ClawGuru is independent. That's exactly why the perspective is operational rather than PR.
faq.a3: Public exposure + no origin/token discipline. That's the classic combo.
faq.a4: Because 90% of damage comes from operations/discipline, not from fancy features.

intel.hero_title: Intel - The Mycelial Threat Intelligence Center
intel.hero_subline: Real-time CVE feeds, AI-driven risk analysis and direct runbook recommendations. Stay one step ahead.
intel.upgrade_text: Full CVE feed, export (CSV/PDF), advanced filters and automatic runbook execution.
intel.free_teaser_text: Only the last 5 CVEs visible. With Day Pass you see the full feed and can export.
intel.analyzer_input_placeholder: CVE ID (e.g. CVE-2024-6387) or keyword (e.g. ssh)
intel.faq_subtitle: Everything you need to know to get the most out of the Intel Center.
intel.faq_a1: Intel is the Mycelial Threat Intelligence Center by ClawGuru. It aggregates real-time CVE feeds, AI-powered risk analysis and direct runbook recommendations – all in one dashboard. Instead of manually searching NIST, GitHub Advisory and OSV, you get operationally prepared threat data here.
intel.faq_a2: DevOps teams, SREs, security engineers and anyone running self-hosted infrastructure (Moltbot, OpenClaw, Docker, Kubernetes, Cloud). Intel helps you identify relevant CVEs instantly and fix them with a matching runbook – no security PhD required.
intel.faq_q3: How does the Live Threat Feed work?
intel.faq_a3: The feed syncs new CVEs every 5 minutes from multiple sources (NVD, OSV, GitHub Advisory). Each entry includes a CVSS score, affected services, severity badge and – if available – a direct link to the matching runbook. Free users see the latest 5 entries; with a Daypass you get the full feed with export.
intel.faq_q4: What does the CVE Analyzer do?
intel.faq_a4: Enter a CVE ID (e.g. CVE-2024-6387) or a keyword (e.g. ssh). The analyzer matches the CVE against your infrastructure tags, calculates a relevance score and immediately recommends the right fix runbook. In the background it uses the Mycelium knowledge graph to detect dependencies and co-occurrence risks.
intel.faq_q5: What is the Predictive Threat Radar?
intel.faq_a5: The radar uses AI (Oracle) to predict CVEs before they are widely exploited. It is based on exploit PoC releases, darknet activity and patch-lag patterns. You see a probability estimate and a recommended timeframe for action.
intel.faq_a6: Mycelium is the ClawGuru knowledge graph. Nodes represent CVEs, services, tags and customer assets. In the preview you see the top clusters of your current threat landscape. The full interactive Mycelium dashboard is available in the Pro/Team plan.
intel.faq_q7: What is included, what costs extra?
intel.faq_a7: Free: last 5 CVEs, CVE Analyzer (limited queries), stats tiles. Daypass: full CVE feed, export (CSV/JSON/PDF), advanced filters, automatic runbook execution. Pro/Team: persistent dashboard data, full Mycelium, API access, audit trail.
intel.faq_a8: No. Intel is exclusively for securing your own systems. All runbooks are defensive – they show how to harden your own infrastructure and close vulnerabilities. No active scanning of third-party systems, no exploit tools.

oracle.hero_subline: See which vulnerabilities will matter next - with concrete runbook recommendations and probabilities.
oracle.how_text: Oracle links current CVE feeds with your technologies and prioritizes the most urgent threats. For each risk you see probabilities, affected services and matching runbooks.
oracle.empty_state: No acute risks detected for the selected scope.
oracle.title: Oracle – Your Predictive Risk Radar
oracle.subtitle: Identify risks before they occur. Personalized predictions for your scope.
oracle.scope_hint: Press Enter to add. Use 3–6 precise terms.

neuro.hero_subline: State your technology stack - ClawGuru finds the most important runbooks, prioritizes by relevance and gives you a clear plan.
neuro.how_text: Neuro compares your technology stack with the tags in our runbook database, scores relevance in % and creates a clear execution plan.
neuro.empty_hint: Select one or more technology tags above to receive personalized runbook recommendations.
neuro.title: Neuro – Your Personal Runbook Guide
neuro.subtitle: Enter your technology stack – ClawGuru finds the most important runbooks, prioritized by relevance, and gives you a clear plan.

summon.hero_subline: Ask across dimensions. We answer with humor and useful runbooks.
summon.input_placeholder: Describe your problem or paste logs...
summon.daypass_hint: Full features with a Day Pass.
summon.callout_strong: This problem can be fixed automatically with a Day Pass — including execution and verification!
summon.how_text: Summon searches the runbook database, matches tags and calculates a confidence score. This gives you the most relevant guides for your specific problem.

mycelium.hero_subline: Explore relationships between runbooks, CVEs, services and best practices.
mycelium.how_text: Every node is a runbook, every edge shows shared tags or affected services.
mycelium.pro_tip_text: Click a node to open the runbook. With a Day Pass you can export the full graph as interactive HTML.

homepage.problem_title: Why SecOps Teams Face Daily Compromises
homepage.problem_sub: Security operations are complex, time-critical, and knowledge-intensive – and most tools only help halfway.
homepage.problem_p1_title: Runbooks exist – but not where you need them
homepage.problem_p1_desc: Confluence, Notion, shared drives. When an incident fires, you lose precious minutes searching – minutes that decide the scale of damage.
homepage.problem_p2_title: Hardening checklists are outdated or missing entirely
homepage.problem_p2_desc: CIS Benchmarks, STIG, BSI IT-Grundschutz: who has the time to keep them current and adapted to the actual stack? Most teams don't.
homepage.problem_p3_title: AI tools give answers – but no executable steps
homepage.problem_p3_desc: ChatGPT explains the problem. But who writes the fix? Who executes it? Who documents the proof for the next audit?
homepage.problem_p4_title: The first 15 minutes of an incident decide the damage
homepage.problem_p4_desc: Breach, data leak, outage: the window for damage control is tight. Without an immediately available plan, every team operates blind under pressure.
homepage.problem_p5_title: Compliance audits mean weeks of manual effort
homepage.problem_p5_desc: NIS2, ISO 27001, SOC 2: evidence and documentation obligations are growing. Teams can't find time for seamless tracking alongside daily operations.
homepage.problem_p6_desc: Junior engineers don't know what to do during incidents. Senior engineers have no time to explain it live. The structural risk remains – as long as knowledge doesn't scale.
homepage.solution_sub: ClawGuru solves the core problem: knowledge where it's needed, in executable form – instantly verifiable.
homepage.solution_p1_desc: Over 4.2 million runbooks, semantically connected. The Engine finds the right path – not just an answer.
homepage.solution_p2_desc: Automated, context-aware, and in your environment. From identification to proof – in seconds.
homepage.solution_p3_desc: Git commit, audit report, certificate. Every execution is documented and traceable.
homepage.tools_sub: From incident response to hardening to compliance – everything in one platform.
homepage.tools_t1_desc: Describe your problem – ClawGuru finds matching runbooks and starts execution.
homepage.tools_t2_desc: Real-time threat intelligence. Automated risk analysis for your infrastructure.
homepage.tools_t3_desc: Live monitoring of your systems. Detection of anomalies and threats in real time.
homepage.tools_t4_desc: Visualized attack surface. See where your vulnerabilities are – before attackers do.
homepage.tools_t5_desc: Automated configuration checks. CIS Benchmarks, STIG, BSI – instantly on your stack.
homepage.tools_t6_desc: AI-powered vulnerability analysis. Proactive identification of risks in code and infrastructure.
homepage.why_sub: We solve the core problem of SecOps: knowledge where it's needed, in executable form – instantly verifiable.
homepage.why_r1_desc: No weeks for onboarding. Productive in 5 minutes – from day one.
homepage.why_r2_desc: 4.2 million runbooks grow with every new requirement. Your knowledge scales automatically.
homepage.why_r3_desc: Every execution is documented. Git commit, audit report, certificate – for the next audit.

roast.title: Roast My Stack
roast.subtitle: Drop your tech stack or a domain. Get a savage-but-useful security roast — then ship fixes with ClawGuru runbooks.
roast.placeholder: Kubernetes + Nginx + AWS + Terraform + Postgres — or app.example.com
roast.disclosure: AI-generated orientation, not a penetration test. Threat model and verify everything in your own environment.
roast.score_hint: Narrative posture score for this roast — still verify in prod.
roast.error_empty_input: Enter a stack or domain first.
roast.error_ai_unavailable: AI is offline. Configure GEMINI_API_KEY (or your AI_PROVIDER_ORDER chain).
roast.error_parse: The model returned junk. Try again with a shorter description.
roast.error_rate_limited: Too many roasts from this connection. Wait a few minutes and try again.
roast.meta_title: Roast My Stack — Free AI security roast | ClawGuru
roast.meta_description: Roast your tech stack with ClawGuru: sharp humor, real SecOps insights, Claw Score, and shareable results — then jump into runbooks and fixes.
roast.page_intro: Built for sharing: one link, one roast, concrete weaknesses and next steps. Not a pentest — verify everything in your environment.
roast.example_1_stack: Nginx 1.24, Ubuntu 22.04, Let's Encrypt, no WAF, no Fail2ban
roast.example_2_stack: K8s 1.29, ArgoCD, Hetzner Cloud, no Network Policy, no Pod Security
roast.example_3_stack: Node 20, Express, PostgreSQL 15, Redis, no rate limiting, JWT without rotation
roast.faq_heading: Frequently asked questions about the stack roast
roast.faq_q1: Is this a real security audit?
roast.faq_a1: No. The roast is an AI-assisted, humorous analysis of public risk indicators. For binding conclusions you need a real pentest.
roast.faq_a2: Your tech stack: software used, versions, cloud provider, network configuration. The more detail, the more precise the findings.
roast.faq_a3: No. Inputs are not stored permanently. The roast runs stateless on EU servers.
roast.faq_q4: What do I do after the roast?
roast.faq_a4: Prioritise the findings, open the matching runbooks and work through them step by step. The roast link is shareable — great for team reviews.

check.page_title: Free Security Check — Claw Score in 30 Seconds
check.page_subtitle: Enter an IP, domain or URL: instantly see which risks are publicly visible — no registration, no agent, no waiting.
check.proof_1_title: Real score, not a marketing number
check.proof_1_desc: The Claw Score is based on real signals: open ports, missing headers, TLS weaknesses. No gamification, no fuzzing.
check.proof_2_title: Fix runbooks right after the scan
check.proof_2_desc: Every finding links to a matching runbook with step-by-step instructions — no copy-paste from Stack Overflow.
check.proof_3_desc: No tracking, no data sharing. Scan targets are not stored permanently. Infrastructure in the EU.
check.score_methodology_heading: How the Claw Score is calculated
check.score_methodology_desc: The score aggregates publicly visible signals in four categories: TLS/HTTPS (25 pts), Security Headers (25 pts), Service Exposure (25 pts), and known CVE hits (25 pts). 100 = perfectly hardened, 0 = critically exposed.
check.methodology_desc: This check evaluates publicly visible signals only (e.g. reachable services, header/TLS indicators, and common exposure patterns). Not a penetration test, no guarantee.
check.methodology_bullet1: Score is heuristic and optimized for fast orientation.
check.methodology_bullet2: For reliable conclusions, always verify configuration, logs, and internal scans.
check.methodology_bullet3: Recommendations are designed for fast hardening execution via runbooks.
check.harden_desc: Most common follow-ups after the check: concrete hardening guides for common stack components.
check.faq_a1: No. The check does not persist targets. Technically required request metadata can appear in server logs.
check.faq_a2: No. It is a fast heuristic evaluation of publicly visible signals. For binding conclusions, validate internally.
check.faq_q3: What should I do after the score?
check.faq_a3: Execute the top recommendations, harden with runbooks, then re-check for improvement.
check.faq_a4: IPv4 addresses, domains (e.g. example.com) and full URLs. The check automatically detects the type and selects the appropriate test modules.
check.faq_a5: As a guest: unlimited for public targets. With an account: prioritised queue, scan history and automatic re-checks.

academy.meta_title: ClawGuru Academy – Free Cyber-Security Courses for Self-Hosters
academy.meta_description: Master OpenClaw, Moltbot and AI Agent Security. Practical hardening guides, incident response playbooks and zero-trust architecture – by self-hosters for self-hosters. Free.
academy.meta_keywords: OpenClaw Hardening, Moltbot Security, Self-Hosted AI Agent Security, Zero-Trust Architecture, Incident Response, Cyber-Security Academy, Self-Hosting Security, Container Security, Network Security
academy.hero_description: Practical hardening guides, incident response playbooks and zero-trust architecture – by self-hosters for self-hosters. Free. No credit card. No bullshit.
academy.what_is_p1: The ClawGuru Academy is the only security platform specifically designed for self-hosters. We don't offer theoretical university courses – we deliver practical, immediately applicable guides that actually make your setup more secure.
academy.what_is_p2: OpenClaw, Moltbot, AI-Agents, Zero-Trust – topics you won't find in conventional security courses. Here you learn from self-hosters who operate and harden these systems daily in production.
academy.what_is_notice: Real-World Focus: No lab scenarios, no demo environments. Everything is based on actual production deployments and real security incidents.
academy.tracks_subtitle: Structured learning paths for every level – from basics to expert
academy.track_openclaw_moltbot_desc: From basics to advanced: Learn how to configure, harden and operate OpenClaw and Moltbot securely from the ground up.
academy.track_ai_agent_security_desc: Secure your AI agents from prompt injection, data exfiltration and supply-chain attacks. Practical defense strategies.
academy.track_zero_trust_desc: Build a true zero-trust infrastructure: mTLS, service mesh, least-privilege IAM and defense-in-depth.
academy.track_incident_response_desc: When things break: detection, triage, containment and recovery. Real-world playbooks for critical incidents.
academy.track_automation_desc: Automate your security: CI/CD pipelines, SBOM generation, image scanning and compliance checks.
academy.courses_subtitle: Start immediately – no signup, no credit card, 100% free
academy.course_openclaw_hardening_desc: Network, auth, containers, secrets – everything you need for a secure OpenClaw deployment.
academy.course_docker_kubernetes_desc: Hardening Docker and Kubernetes: Seccomp, AppArmor, network policies and image scanning.
academy.course_prompt_injection_desc: Secure AI agents from prompt injection: input sanitization, tool scope limits and sandboxing.
academy.course_network_firewall_desc: iptables, nftables, WireGuard and zero-trust networking – practical configuration.
academy.course_supply_chain_desc: SBOM, Cosign, Sigstore and dependency pinning – prevent supply-chain attacks.
academy.course_llm_gateway_desc: Secure your self-hosted Ollama/LocalAI gateway: authentication, rate limiting, audit logging and network isolation.
academy.course_model_poisoning_desc: Defend AI models against data poisoning, backdoor attacks and supply chain threats in training pipelines.
academy.course_incident_response_desc: When it hits: detection, containment, recovery and postmortem – step by step.
academy.course_cve_feed_desc: Latest CVEs with actionable fix guides for OpenSSH, Docker, Next.js and more — sorted by severity.
academy.resources_subtitle: Tools, downloads and checklists – everything you need for a secure setup
academy.resource_check_desc: Live HTTP header analysis and security score
academy.community_subtitle: By self-hosters for self-hosters – real stories from production
academy.story_1_title: How we hardened OpenClaw for 10,000+ users
academy.story_1_excerpt: A real case: from unprotected admin ports to a complete zero-trust architecture in 6 weeks.
academy.story_2_title: Prompt-Injection Attack in Production – Lessons Learned
academy.story_2_excerpt: How we detected and blocked a prompt-injection attack on our AI agent.
academy.final_cta_desc: Start now with a free live security check or download our hardening checklists.
academy.final_cta_note: No signup required. 100% free. No credit card.
